details: Chai-as-const is a combosquat of the popular chai assertion library. When the exported middleware function is called, it spawns a detached background Node.js process that POSTs all environment variables (including any NPM tokens, GitHub tokens, AWS credentials, and other secrets) to a C2 endpoint at hxxps://ipcheck-hashed[.]vercel[.]app/api/auth/b4dadd6a26d820d08596, then executes the server's response as arbitrary code via the Function constructor — enabling remote code execution on the installer's machine. chai-as-const is a combosquat of the chai testing library. When required, it spawns a detached background process that POSTs all environment variables (including credentials, tokens, and API keys) to a remote C2 endpoint at ipcheck-hashed[.]vercel[.]app/api/auth/b4dadd6a26d820d08596, then executes arbitrary JavaScript code returned by the server. The package has no repository URL and its description is a copy-pasted vulnerability management document unrelated to its functionality.
